Iteration 0: Base program full valset score: 0.8823529411764706 over 34 / 34 examples

litellm.exceptions.RateLimitError: litellm.RateLimitError: RateLimitError: OpenAIException - Too many requests. For more on scraping GitHub and how it may affect your rights, please review our Terms of Service (https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/github-terms/github-terms-of-service).

Iteration 1: Reflective mutation did not propose a new candidate
Iteration 2: Selected program 0 score: 0.8823529411764706
Iteration 2: Proposed new text for current_candidate: ---
name: azure-artifacts
description: 'UTILITY SKILL — Source of truth for artifact templates, H2 heading compliance, and documentation styling across Steps 1–7. WHEN: generate artifact; artifact template; H2 compliance; check H2 structure; as-built/Step 7 docs; docs styling; update project README; run artifact lint/validation. Use to produce structured outputs from step templates and to validate heading order. Not for infrastructure configuration, IaC patterns, or environment provisioning.'
compatibility: Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, VS Code, and any Agent Skills compatible tool.
license: MIT
metadata:
  author: jonathan-vella
  version: "2.1"
  category: workflow-automation
---

# Azure Artifacts Skill

Single source of truth for artifact template structures, required H2 headings, and styling standards. Per-step definitions live in references/; load only the step you are generating.

## Rules

- Use this skill to:
  - Produce step artifacts from the matching template
  - Enforce required H2 text and order
  - Apply documentation styling standards
  - Update the project README after artifact creation
- Do not use this skill to:
  - Provision or configure infrastructure
  - Design infrastructure-as-code patterns
  - Build CI/CD pipelines or manage environment/secrets

Mandatory compliance:
- Read the correct step template from references/ and replicate its required H2s
- Copy H2 text verbatim (including emoji) and keep the listed order
- Additional sections are allowed only after the last required H2
- Do not omit any required H2
- Include attribution: > Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}
- Save outputs under agent-output/{project}/ using the prescribed filenames

Operational practices:
- Read the template before generating
- Replace all {placeholder} tokens; never leave “TBD” or similar
- Do not reorder or rename required H2 headings
- Do not add custom H2s before the final required H2

Project README (mandatory):
- Each agent-output/{project}/ must contain README.md
- After saving artifacts:
  - Mark the step complete in “Workflow Progress”
  - List files in “Generated Artifacts”
  - Update “Last Updated” and the progress percentage

## Steps

1) Read template: references/0N-*-template.md for the target step
2) Copy H2 skeleton: replicate every required H2 in exact text and order
3) Fill content: resolve all placeholders; never use “TBD”
4) Add attribution: > Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}
5) Save file: agent-output/{project}/ with the prescribed filename
6) Update README: progress, artifact list, date, and progress bar
7) Validate: run npm run lint:artifact-templates and npm run lint:h2-sync

Revisions (targeted edits):
- First draft uses create_file
- All later edits must be targeted: batch all accepted changes from a review pass into one multi_replace_string_in_file call
- See references/revision-workflow.md for detailed procedure and the structural rewrite exception

## MCP Tools

- fs.readFile: load templates from references/
- fs.create_file: write initial artifact drafts
- fs.multi_replace_string_in_file: batch revisions after review
- fs.writeFile / fs.exists / glob.find: manage outputs and discover templates
- shell.run (or command.run): execute validators (npm run lint:artifact-templates, npm run lint:h2-sync, npm run validate:all)
- datetime.now: stamp attribution date

## Placeholder Syntax

- Single braces only: {placeholder-name}
- Lowercase, hyphen-separated tokens (for example: {project-name}, {monthly-cost})
- Do not use double braces

## Automated Validation

- npm run lint:artifact-templates  — validates required H2 text and order
- npm run lint:h2-sync            — checks template ↔ artifact synchronization
- npm run validate:all            — runs all validators

## Quality Checklist

- Required H2 headings match template exactly (text and order)
- Attribution present with agent name and date
- No unresolved placeholders or “TBD”
- File saved under agent-output/{project}/ with correct name
- Collapsible TOC present if the template includes it
- Cross-navigation header present if the template includes it
- Mermaid diagram present if required by the template
- Traffic-light status indicators used where specified
- Collapsible details blocks included where specified

## Reference Index

- references/01-requirements-template.md — Step 1 requirements
- references/02-architecture-template.md — Step 2 assessment / Step 3 cost estimate
- references/04-plan-template.md — Step 4 plan, governance, preflight
- references/05-code-template.md — Step 5 implementation reference
- references/06-deploy-template.md — Step 6 deployment summary
- references/07-docs-template.md — Step 7 workload documentation
- references/styling-standards.md — Callouts, badges, emoji, navigation
- references/cost-estimate-sections.md — Cost estimate H2 structure and formatting
- references/revision-workflow.md — Targeted-edit procedure
Iteration 2: New subsample score 2.573529411764706 is not better than old score 2.6470588235294117, skipping
Iteration 3: Selected program 0 score: 0.8823529411764706
Iteration 3: Proposed new text for current_candidate: ---
name: azure-artifacts
description: '**UTILITY SKILL** — Artifact template structures, H2 compliance rules, and documentation styling for agent outputs (Steps 1–7). WHEN: "generate artifact", "artifact template", "check H2 structure", "validate H2 order", "lint artifact headings", "step 7 as-built". USE FOR: producing step-based artifacts from templates, enforcing exact H2 text/order, running artifact lint commands, and targeted revision workflows. NOT FOR: cloud resource setup, infrastructure code patterns, or environment configuration.'
compatibility: Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, VS Code, and any Agent Skills compatible tool.
license: MIT
metadata:
  author: jonathan-vella
  version: "2.1"
  category: workflow-automation
---

# Artifacts Skill

Source of truth for artifact templates, H2 compliance, and documentation styling. Per-step definitions live in references/. Load only the template for the step you are generating.

## Rules

- Read the step-specific template from references/ before generating any artifact.
- Copy required H2 headings exactly (text, emoji, punctuation) and keep the listed order.
- Do not omit required H2s. Extra sections are allowed only after the last required H2.
- Replace all {placeholder} tokens; never leave TBD/Insert here or similar filler.
- Add attribution line at the end: > Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}
- Save outputs under agent-output/{project}/ using the prescribed filenames in the template.
- Ensure agent-output/{project}/README.md exists. After each artifact:
  - Mark the step complete in Workflow Progress.
  - List the generated files in Generated Artifacts.
  - Update Last Updated date and progress indicator.
- Revisions:
  - Initial draft: create_file.
  - All subsequent edits: use a single multi_replace_string_in_file call per review pass bundling all accepted fixes.
  - Only use a structural rewrite (new file + replace) if the template version changed or the structure must be re-synced.

## Steps

1. Identify the target step (N) and project name.
2. Load the matching template:
   - references/01-requirements-template.md
   - references/02-architecture-template.md
   - references/04-plan-template.md
   - references/05-code-template.md
   - references/06-deploy-template.md
   - references/07-docs-template.md
   - Also consult references/styling-standards.md as needed.
3. Extract the required H2 skeleton and replicate it exactly in the same order.
4. Fill content:
   - Replace every {placeholder-name}.
   - Include diagrams, tables, callouts, traffic-light indicators, and details blocks where the template instructs.
   - Follow styling-standards for badges, TOC, navigation, and mermaid usage if applicable.
5. Add the attribution line: > Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}
6. Save the file under agent-output/{project}/ using the filename pattern specified in the template.
7. Update agent-output/{project}/README.md:
   - Mark the step complete, list artifacts, refresh the Last Updated date and progress.
8. Validate:
   - If shell available: run npm run lint:artifact-templates and npm run lint:h2-sync (or npm run validate:all).
   - If shell unavailable: manually verify H2 text/order, presence of all required sections, no placeholders, correct path/filename, and required styling elements from the template.
9. Revisions workflow:
   - Collect all accepted findings from a review pass (challenger/user decisions/approvals).
   - Map them to precise string replacements.
   - Apply one multi_replace_string_in_file call covering all changes.
   - Re-run validation.

## MCP Tools

- File I/O:
  - read_file: load templates from references/ and existing artifacts/README.
  - create_file: initial artifact drafts and README if missing.
  - write_file or append_file: updates to README or artifact rewrites when required.
- Targeted edits:
  - multi_replace_string_in_file: bundle all accepted fixes into a single call per review pass.
- Shell/commands (optional):
  - execute_command or shell.run to invoke:
    - npm run lint:artifact-templates
    - npm run lint:h2-sync
    - npm run validate:all
  - If unavailable, perform the manual checks described in Steps → Validate.
Iteration 3: New subsample score 2.8235294117647056 is better than old score 2.6470588235294117. Continue to full eval and add to candidate pool.
Iteration 3: Found a better program on the valset with score 0.9411764705882353.

Iteration 3: New program candidate index: 1
{
  "skill": "azure-artifacts",
  "original": "# Azure Artifacts Skill\n\nSingle source of truth for artifact template structures and styling.\nPer-step H2 definitions live in `references/` \u2014 load only the step\nyou are generating.\n\n## Rules\n\n### Mandatory Compliance\n\n| Rule                  | Requirement                                         |\n| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |\n| **Template skeleton** | Read step template from `references/` and replicate |\n| **Exact text**        | Use H2 text from templates verbatim                 |\n| **Exact order**       | Required H2s appear in the order listed             |\n| **Anchor rule**       | Extra sections allowed ONLY after last required H2  |\n| **No omissions**      | Every H2 listed must appear in output               |\n| **Attribution**       | `> Generated by {agent} agent \\| {YYYY-MM-DD}`      |\n\n### DO / DON'T\n\n- **DO**: Read the step-specific template before generating\n- **DO**: Copy H2 text character-for-character (including emoji)\n- **DO**: Save all output to `agent-output/{project}/`\n- **DON'T**: Reorder H2 headings from the listed sequence\n- **DON'T**: Use placeholder text like \"TBD\" or \"Insert here\"\n- **DON'T**: Add custom H2 sections BEFORE the last required H2\n\n## Mandatory: Project README\n\nEvery project in `agent-output/{project}/` **MUST** have a\n`README.md`.\n\nAfter saving step artifact(s), update the README:\n\n1. Mark your step as **complete** in `## \u2705 Workflow Progress`\n2. Add artifact files to `## \ud83d\udcc4 Generated Artifacts`\n3. Update `Last Updated` date and progress bar percentage\n\n## Steps\n\nArtifact generation flow (per Step N):\n\n1. **Read template** \u2014 load the matching `references/0N-*-template.md` for the step you are generating\n2. **Copy H2 skeleton** \u2014 replicate every required H2 in the listed order, character-for-character\n3. **Fill content** \u2014 replace `{placeholder}` tokens; never use \"TBD\" or \"Insert here\"\n4. **Add attribution** \u2014 `> Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}`\n5. **Save** to `agent-output/{project}/` with the prescribed filename\n6. **Update README** \u2014 mark step complete, list artifacts, refresh `Last Updated` date\n7. **Validate** \u2014 run `npm run lint:artifact-templates` and `npm run lint:h2-sync`\n\nFor revisions (challenger findings, user-decision Apply/Skip/Defer, approval-gate fixes), see [`references/revision-workflow.md`](./references/revision-workflow.md) \u2014 bundle all fixes into a single `multi_replace_string_in_file` call.\n\n## Revision Workflow (Targeted Edits)\n\n**Initial draft** uses `create_file`. **All subsequent revisions** MUST use targeted edit tools. Bundle every accepted fix from a review pass into a single `multi_replace_string_in_file` call (a 24-finding revision is one tool call, not 24). Full procedure, rationale, and structural-rewrite exception in [`references/revision-workflow.md`](./references/revision-workflow.md).\n\n## Placeholder Syntax\n\nAll templates use single-brace `{placeholder-name}` syntax:\n\n- Lowercase, hyphen-separated: `{project-name}`, `{monthly-cost}`\n- No Mustache/Handlebars `{{double-braces}}`\n\n## Automated Validation\n\n```bash\nnpm run lint:artifact-templates   # H2 order and required headings\nnpm run lint:h2-sync              # Template \u2194 artifact sync\nnpm run validate:all              # All validators together\n```\n\n## Quality Checklist\n\n- [ ] H2 headings match template exactly (text + order)\n- [ ] Attribution header present with agent name and date\n- [ ] No placeholder text (e.g. \"TBD\", \"Insert here\", task markers)\n- [ ] File saved to `agent-output/{project}/` with correct name\n- [ ] Collapsible TOC present (`<details open>` block after badge row)\n- [ ] Cross-navigation table present (header with \u2b05\ufe0f Previous / \ud83d\udcd1 Index / Next \u27a1\ufe0f)\n- [ ] Mermaid diagram included (if template contains one)\n- [ ] Traffic-light indicators used (\u2705 / \u26a0\ufe0f / \u274c where template shows status columns)\n- [ ] Collapsible `<details>` blocks present (if template contains them)\n\n## Reference Index\n\nWhen generating a Step N artifact, read the corresponding template:\n\n| Reference                                | When to Load                                         |\n| ---------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n| `references/01-requirements-template.md` | Generating Step 1 requirements                       |\n| `references/02-architecture-template.md` | Generating Step 2 assessment or Step 3 cost estimate |\n| `references/04-plan-template.md`         | Generating Step 4 plan, governance, or preflight     |\n| `references/05-code-template.md`         | Generating Step 5 implementation reference           |\n| `references/06-deploy-template.md`       | Generating Step 6 deployment summary                 |\n| `references/07-docs-template.md`         | Generating Step 7 workload documentation             |\n| `references/styling-standards.md`        | Applying callouts, badges, emoji, navigation         |\n| `references/cost-estimate-sections.md`   | Cost estimate H2 structure and formatting rules      |\n| `references/revision-workflow.md`        | Detailed targeted-edit revision procedure (Step 7+)  |",
  "optimized": "---\nname: azure-artifacts\ndescription: '**UTILITY SKILL** \u2014 Artifact template structures, H2 compliance rules, and documentation styling for agent outputs (Steps 1\u20137). WHEN: \"generate artifact\", \"artifact template\", \"check H2 structure\", \"validate H2 order\", \"lint artifact headings\", \"step 7 as-built\". USE FOR: producing step-based artifacts from templates, enforcing exact H2 text/order, running artifact lint commands, and targeted revision workflows. NOT FOR: cloud resource setup, infrastructure code patterns, or environment configuration.'\ncompatibility: Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, VS Code, and any Agent Skills compatible tool.\nlicense: MIT\nmetadata:\n  author: jonathan-vella\n  version: \"2.1\"\n  category: workflow-automation\n---\n\n# Artifacts Skill\n\nSource of truth for artifact templates, H2 compliance, and documentation styling. Per-step definitions live in references/. Load only the template for the step you are generating.\n\n## Rules\n\n- Read the step-specific template from references/ before generating any artifact.\n- Copy required H2 headings exactly (text, emoji, punctuation) and keep the listed order.\n- Do not omit required H2s. Extra sections are allowed only after the last required H2.\n- Replace all {placeholder} tokens; never leave TBD/Insert here or similar filler.\n- Add attribution line at the end: > Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}\n- Save outputs under agent-output/{project}/ using the prescribed filenames in the template.\n- Ensure agent-output/{project}/README.md exists. After each artifact:\n  - Mark the step complete in Workflow Progress.\n  - List the generated files in Generated Artifacts.\n  - Update Last Updated date and progress indicator.\n- Revisions:\n  - Initial draft: create_file.\n  - All subsequent edits: use a single multi_replace_string_in_file call per review pass bundling all accepted fixes.\n  - Only use a structural rewrite (new file + replace) if the template version changed or the structure must be re-synced.\n\n## Steps\n\n1. Identify the target step (N) and project name.\n2. Load the matching template:\n   - references/01-requirements-template.md\n   - references/02-architecture-template.md\n   - references/04-plan-template.md\n   - references/05-code-template.md\n   - references/06-deploy-template.md\n   - references/07-docs-template.md\n   - Also consult references/styling-standards.md as needed.\n3. Extract the required H2 skeleton and replicate it exactly in the same order.\n4. Fill content:\n   - Replace every {placeholder-name}.\n   - Include diagrams, tables, callouts, traffic-light indicators, and details blocks where the template instructs.\n   - Follow styling-standards for badges, TOC, navigation, and mermaid usage if applicable.\n5. Add the attribution line: > Generated by {agent} agent | {YYYY-MM-DD}\n6. Save the file under agent-output/{project}/ using the filename pattern specified in the template.\n7. Update agent-output/{project}/README.md:\n   - Mark the step complete, list artifacts, refresh the Last Updated date and progress.\n8. Validate:\n   - If shell available: run npm run lint:artifact-templates and npm run lint:h2-sync (or npm run validate:all).\n   - If shell unavailable: manually verify H2 text/order, presence of all required sections, no placeholders, correct path/filename, and required styling elements from the template.\n9. Revisions workflow:\n   - Collect all accepted findings from a review pass (challenger/user decisions/approvals).\n   - Map them to precise string replacements.\n   - Apply one multi_replace_string_in_file call covering all changes.\n   - Re-run validation.\n\n## MCP Tools\n\n- File I/O:\n  - read_file: load templates from references/ and existing artifacts/README.\n  - create_file: initial artifact drafts and README if missing.\n  - write_file or append_file: updates to README or artifact rewrites when required.\n- Targeted edits:\n  - multi_replace_string_in_file: bundle all accepted fixes into a single call per review pass.\n- Shell/commands (optional):\n  - execute_command or shell.run to invoke:\n    - npm run lint:artifact-templates\n    - npm run lint:h2-sync\n    - npm run validate:all\n  - If unavailable, perform the manual checks described in Steps \u2192 Validate.",
  "best_score": null
}
